lemma_statement.name: restricted_continuation_energy_inequality_discharge_lemma
lemma_statement.claim: On the restricted-continuation region, the restricted energy inequality is certified whenever the explicitly listed energy identity, boundary-flux sign/coercivity, source-estimate, and no-positive-remainder hypotheses are supplied.

required_inputs.name: restricted_continuation_energy_identity
required_inputs.role: supplies the certified restricted energy identity

required_inputs.name: restricted_continuation_boundary_flux_sign_control_exists
required_inputs.role: supplies the sign control needed to place the boundary flux term on the dissipative side

required_inputs.name: restricted_continuation_boundary_flux_coercivity_exists
required_inputs.role: supplies the quantitative coercive boundary control used in the inequality

required_inputs.name: restricted_source_pairing_upper_bound_available
required_inputs.role: bounds the source/interior forcing term by admissible energy quantities

required_inputs.name: energy_identity_remainders_are_nonpositive_or_absent
required_inputs.role: ensures every non-certified remainder is either absent or has the correct sign

required_inputs.name: energy_inequality_orientation_convention_compatible
required_inputs.role: ensures the inequality direction matches the restricted energy identity and boundary orientation conventions

discharged_obligations: Separates the energy inequality from the energy identity.
Separates the energy inequality from boundary flux sign control and coercivity.
Certifies the exact assumptions under which the restricted-continuation energy inequality is available.
Promotes the energy inequality from an undischarged target obligation to a conditional discharge lemma.
non_claims: Does not prove continuation or contradiction.
Does not prove uniform restricted continuation bounds.
Does not prove full RR closure.
